task_completion.description: Open the specified scene in OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application is open, and the title bar, scene browser, or Xsheet indicates that 'dwanko_run.tnz' is loaded.

task_completion.description: Create a new Toonz Vector Level (PLI).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Level' dialog showing 'Toonz Vector Level' selected, or the Xsheet/Level Strip showing a new level with a vector icon (typically a small yellow/green shape icon or .pli extension).

task_completion.description: Position the new vector level in a column that renders above the character animation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenToonz Xsheet, the new vector level is placed in a column to the right of the character animation column (which makes it render on top), or in the Timeline view, it is placed in a layer above the character.

task_completion.description: Draw a red circle guide framing the character on the new level.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewer shows a red circle drawn around the character. The color palette/style editor should show a red color selected, and the Geometry/Shape tool is likely active.

task_completion.description: Render Frame 1 of the scene as a PNG to the specified output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog shows the save path '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/guide_overlay/' and format 'png', OR a file explorer/terminal window shows the rendered PNG file present in that directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through OpenToonz menus, using the drawing tools, and configuring render settings, rather than using terminal scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent actually drew the circle using OpenToonz tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Geometry/Circle tool is selected in the OpenToonz toolbar and applied to the canvas, rather than importing a pre-drawn image of a red circle.

integrity.description: The agent used OpenToonz to render the final image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz render dialog, progress bar, or output settings are visible, proving the image wasn't generated via an external tool like ImageMagick or Python.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Scene path: /home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz. Output directory: /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/guide_overlay. Target color: Red (RGB [255, 0, 0]). The rendered image must contain more than 200 red pixels to be considered valid.
